raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation likely asks for checking whether the contribution framing, interface-vs-modeling claim, and general thesis are coherent.","R1.2":"A general check of this passage centrally involves testing whether the argument moves logically from interface design to study results to contribution claims.","R1.3":"The passage makes empirical claims from participant logs and self-reports, so checking claim support against the reported evidence is central.","R1.4":"The artifact contains multiple firstness and foundational-contribution claims, making novelty assessment a central expected responsibility.","R1.6":"A general check of a paper passage normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection must account for uncertainty because the delegation is terse and does not specify the exact kind of checking requested.","RX.2":"The artifact contains broad claims about firstness, generalization, and foundational impact, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The projection must remain limited to responsibility assignment and must not perform the requested check itself.","RX.4":"Checking research claims can affect downstream scholarly interpretation and practitioner adoption, so harm from misleading validation must be avoided.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact features that motivate the active responsibilities."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation likely asks for checking whether the contribution framing, interface-vs-modeling claim, and general thesis are coherent.
rationale.R1.2: A general check of this passage centrally involves testing whether the argument moves logically from interface design to study results to contribution claims.
rationale.R1.3: The passage makes empirical claims from participant logs and self-reports, so checking claim support against the reported evidence is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact contains multiple firstness and foundational-contribution claims, making novelty assessment a central expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A general check of a paper passage normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must account for uncertainty because the delegation is terse and does not specify the exact kind of checking requested.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains broad claims about firstness, generalization, and foundational impact, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must remain limited to responsibility assignment and must not perform the requested check itself.
rationale.RX.4: Checking research claims can affect downstream scholarly interpretation and practitioner adoption, so harm from misleading validation must be avoided.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact features that motivate the active responsibilities.
